element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Roll button mechanic for evasion introduced immediately for player control

element_introduction_order.element: Basic enemy fighters (Kawasaki Ki-61, Mitsubishi A6M Zero)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn basic dodge and shoot patterns

element_introduction_order.element: Power-up: Escort formation (Tip Tow - two smaller fighters)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Increases firepower and visual complexity; reward for survival
Requires sustained fire and advanced evasion
Final test of accumulated skills and power-up effectiveness

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe screen areas with fewer enemies
Escort fighter acquisition reducing player vulnerability
typical_tension_triggers: Dense enemy bullet patterns requiring evasion
Loss of escort formation after taking damage
Screen scrolling bringing new enemy waves into view
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous escalating difficulty with periodic relief through power-up acquisition; vertically scrolling design creates constant forward momentum and new threat introduction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible design with manageable enemy density; teaches core shoot and dodge mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy variety and formation complexity; power-up acquisition creates strategic depth
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained bullet patterns requiring mastery of roll evasion mechanic; heavier aircraft introduce durability pressure
notable_spikes: Introduction of Kawasaki Ki-48 heavy fighters requiring sustained firepower
Boss encounter as final gauntlet testing accumulated skill and power-up state

notable_level_patterns.description: Game continuously scrolls vertically; players navigate through Pacific Theater airspace with increasing enemy opposition; single-pass level design with no backtracking
Enemies appear in coordinated formations that scroll into view; players must predict and position for optimal targeting while maintaining evasion
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game stages with Ki-61 and Zero formations

notable_level_patterns.description: Escort fighter power-up and other items appear after defeating enemy groups, creating risk-reward incentive to engage rather than purely evade

sources_used: Wikipedia: 1942 (video game) - Gameplay and Design sections
The Cutting Room Floor: 1942 (NES) - Developer and port information
